name: The White House
subject: One family’s letter to President Trump
body:

## The Day Ahead
| • | Vice President Mike Pence will address the situation in Venezuela at a meeting of the Organization of American States in Washington. [Watch live at 1:25 p.m. ET.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=17c94ed27d26f1a48c143f9e7bb319657171d805c34aebced41c48599e2abe88b0b1d18e33a4e4d24f1cc8cc77ee86729d2486b99d0ba7b8)
---|---
• | First Lady Melania Trump will hold a launch event to announce her core initiative as First Lady. [Watch live at 3:00 p.m. ET.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=17c94ed27d26f1a48c143f9e7bb319657171d805c34aebced41c48599e2abe88b0b1d18e33a4e4d24f1cc8cc77ee86729d2486b99d0ba7b8)

---
|

## One family’s letter to President Trump
**President Donald J. Trump was in Cleveland, Ohio, on Saturday** , talking with local residents about the impact of his December tax cuts legislation on jobs and wages. As of March, Cleveland alone has gained 3,300 new manufacturing jobs since the Trump Administration took office.
**Working families across Ohio—and the Nation—have shared their stories** with President Trump in recent months about the effect of tax cuts on their lives. Saturday was no different. Sharlene Thornton, a resident of the Ohio Valley, spoke about the difference several hundred dollars per paycheck can make for families working hard to get by.
**“The reason I’m here is because I wrote a letter to you,”** she told the President. “And I didn’t really think it was going to be read, but it was. They’re listening,” Mrs. Thornton said to the crowd in Cleveland.
[Read Sharlene Thornton’s letter to President Trump.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=17c94ed27d26f1a4eb0c5464a2867584d752828ca7c1bcd07d3731ff7ca194cb36c634537cf575145bd94482040b165f9b77cc7ce1a74f24)
**Watch** : [The President talks with Ohioans about tax relief and the Trump economy. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=17c94ed27d26f1a4cd6490f48a49a47e54617d5d8d5e20dfbfd28d59c3a6d275d8528dff365ed20c893df544c47bb16646e0c0f3768c4076)

---
|

## Unemployment below 4 percent
**More good news about the economy broke on Friday** ahead of President Trump’s Ohio trip. The Bureau of Labor Statistics announced in its April jobs report that the U.S. added 164,000 new jobs last month across sectors, including manufacturing (24,000 new jobs), construction (17,000), and mining and logging (8,000).
**The unemployment rate broke below 4 percent for the first time since 2000** with all those new jobs. It also fell far below the pre-recession average of 5.3 percent.
**Many groups that have struggled historically are seeing their prospects improve**. The African-American unemployment rate hit its lowest average ever recorded, and the unemployment rate for adult women, 3.5 percent, is at its lowest level in more than 17 years.
[See what you need to know about the April jobs report.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=17c94ed27d26f1a40b9c0e07ab3e6d8447bf4665a87a9ce08493ee78538d6dd6d321e6bd65c46e4246e2d7a59ece40369427fc16dc91da4a)

---
|

name: Bobby Kaple for Congress
subject: This House seat has to flip
body:
Friend -

I'm Bobby Kaple. I live in Milton, Georgia with my wife Rebecca, our twins, and our dog. You may know me as a leading anchor on the morning news — but I am pleased to share with you that I am now running for Congress in Georgia's Sixth Congressional District, and I want your support.

http://www.bobbykaple.com [http://www.bobbykaple.com]This is one of the most competitive districts in the country. The path to retaking the House goes right through Georgia's Sixth, and our campaign is leading the charge to take it back.

Healthcare is personal to me. My twins came into the world in Atlanta at 3 pounds each, and my wife and I had to spend more than 17 days in the Neonatal Intensive Care Unit at Piedmont Hospital.

In Congress, I'll fight passionately to make sure every American has access to affordable healthcare, and I will never sit by and let Washington politicians take us back to the days of abandoning middle-class families in order to cozy up to special interest donors.

Here's how you can help and join this fight:

*

—

I started my career as a gofer in TV and radio and worked my way up to the anchor desk here in Atlanta. My career has taken me all over this country, but my wife and I have always known we wanted to raise our kids right here in Milton.

Georgia is my home. Like you, I've had it with career politicians, which is why I'm committed to making this community a better place for not only my children, but for everyone who lives here. In Congress, I'll work with members of both parties to get things done.

Let's work together to end the dysfunction in Washington. I hope you'll join me and my family in this effort. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bk-welcome?refcode=BK_EM_WELCOME_WEBSIGNUP_2018&amount=20.00&recurring=true]

Thank you,

Bobby

When Tom Wolf ran his kitchen cabinet and home products company, he invested in his workers by providing training programs, profit sharing, and excellent benefits. He did it because it was the right thing to do -- and because it's smart business.

As governor, Tom has brought that same, worker-first mentality to Harrisburg by investing in job training and workforce development programs so Pennsylvanians can get the good-paying, family-supporting jobs that create a healthy economy.

Since Tom took office, he's increased the number of students enrolled in career and technical education programs by 42 percent and launched nearly 100 new apprenticeship programs.

name: Joe Donnelly
subject: Are you ready to vote?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Early voter turnout is already up from past primaries! Make sure you don't miss out yourself. When everybody votes, Indiana is stronger. |
---

| | Peter,

Tomorrow is an election day, and I want to make sure you're ready to vote (if you haven't already).

You can check where your polling place is at [**IndianaVoters.com**](www.indianavoters.com). Make sure you bring a government-issued ID (Indiana Driver's License, Indiana Photo ID card, passport, or military ID). Polls are open from 6 a.m. to 6 p.m.

Early voter turnout is already up from past primaries! Make sure you don't miss out yourself. When everybody votes, Indiana is stronger.

I've spent the past five days driving the RV around the state to hear from as many voters as possible. Voters from Evansville to South Bend have told me that we need more Hoosier common sense in Washington. It's exciting to see how fired up our grassroots supporters are to keep fighting for Indiana.

Tomorrow, we'll also find out who we're up against in the general election. There's been a nasty primary on the other side, but after that ends tomorrow, all of their firepower will be redirected and aimed squarely at me.

**Will you make a contribution to my campaign so we have the resources ready to fight back immediately?** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
---
**[Express donate $10>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c209c/7377f838/3f007103/51f97dd3/3093159302/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-gotvprimary?express_lane=true&refcode=20180507.1nd&amount=10")**
**[Express donate $25>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c209c/7377f838/3f007103/51f97dd2/3093159302/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-gotvprimary?express_lane=true&refcode=20180507.1nd&amount=25")**
**[Express donate $50>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c209c/7377f838/3f007103/51f97dcd/3093159302/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-gotvprimary?express_lane=true&refcode=20180507.1nd&amount=50")**
**[Express donate $100>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c209c/7377f838/3f007103/51f97dcc/3093159302/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-gotvprimary?express_lane=true&refcode=20180507.1nd&amount=100")**
**[Or, donate a different amount>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c209c/7377f838/3f007103/51f97dcf/3093159302/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-gotvprimary?refcode=20180507.1nd")**
This election is about fighting for fairness for Hoosiers -- defending access to affordable health care, protecting Social Security, keeping our commitments to our seniors and military veterans, and making sure that we have a Senate that works for hard-working Hoosiers, not D.C. special interest donors.

Let's make sure we win that fight by making our voices heard. Make a plan to vote, and take a friend, colleague, neighbor, or family member with you!

Happy voting,
Joe

subject: Voting Starts Today!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
Peter,
Early voting starts today in Iowa!
Today marks the day that voters here are able to vote early and request absentee ballots. **An impressive primary showing is key to demonstrating the strength of our campaign as we move toward the fight against Steve King in the general election.** We’re busy knocking on doors, making phone calls, and getting out to talk with voters across the district between now and June 5. [**_Help us collect the resources to connect with as many voters as possible by making a contribution today!_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1171307/6497379/795583278?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWY3N2Q5MC0zZDUyLWU4MTEtODBjMy0wMDE1NWRhNzgwMTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-VbEcsJ6VbEU_QAyJ8Fn22xxwztLhJ4KiXU1Yp0prvI=)
As a working class candidate, I believe in the old school style of politics: hard work, getting out to the people, listening and earning their vote. I’ve said many times on the trail that you won’t find a candidate that works harder than me and you won’t find a candidate that has more fun than me. What isn’t fun about campaigning? Repeatedly asking folks for money, but campaign literature, yard signs, and mailings aren’t cheap. Your support will help deliver our message to the people of Iowa’s 4th District. [**_Say you’re in with a contribution of any size today._**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1171308/6497380/795583278?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWY3N2Q5MC0zZDUyLWU4MTEtODBjMy0wMDE1NWRhNzgwMTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-VbEcsJ6VbEU_QAyJ8Fn22xxwztLhJ4KiXU1Yp0prvI=)
**I’m putting all I have into this race because the people of this district deserve nothing less.** Your continued support makes all of this possible. Thank you!
Standing Tall for All,
J.D. Scholten

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: #WakandaTheVote Forever
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30d7/9e00690/44f6e274/2402150e/4249893624/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180507_WaVote_fnd_gq_act_1_AG&source=em18_180507_WaVote_fnd_gq_act_1_AG")
Friends --

When _Black Panther_ hit theaters, it became one of the highest grossing movies in America -- and it's clear to see why it's become such a cultural phenomenon.

Not only was it historic in having an African-American director and predominantly Black cast, it tackled themes of race and identity head-on, rather than sidestepping it. All while representing black men and women in positive ways the silver screen has rarely shown.

And in turn, _Black Panther_ inspired #WakandaTheVote, a political movement dedicated to empowering Black voters.

In Florida, we have the chance to make history this November -- but everyone needs to make their voice heard in order to make that happen. **[So we're joining the #WakandaTheVote effort -- and with it, a new bumper sticker to help spread the word. Chip in whatever you can afford to get yours right now!](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30d7/9e00690/44f6e274/24021501/4249893624/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-wakanda-the-vote?gpsid=13_55_344&refcode=em18_180507_WaVote_fnd_gq_act_1_AG&utm_campaign=em18_180507_WaVote_fnd_gq_act_1_AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=2+-+So+wersquore+joining+the+WakandaTheVote+")**
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30d7/9e00690/44f6e274/24021500/4249893624/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-wakanda-the-vote?gpsid=13_55_344&refcode=em18_180507_WaVote_fnd_gq_act_1_AG&utm_campaign=em18_180507_WaVote_fnd_gq_act_1_AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=3")
---
Just like Wakanda, we need to defend our communities and collectively work to improve it from within. We can all be superheroes, and we can all be activists for change. Together, we're proving that when we mobilize, we can truly change the outcome of elections, change the government, and change our future for the better.

**[So please, chip in whatever you can afford to get your sticker and help #WakandaTheVote this year.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30d7/9e00690/44f6e274/24021503/4249893624/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-wakanda-the-vote?gpsid=13_55_344&refcode=em18_180507_WaVote_fnd_gq_act_1_AG&utm_campaign=em18_180507_WaVote_fnd_gq_act_1_AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=4+-+So+please+chip+in+whatever+you+can+affor")**

Bring it home,

Andrew

name: Mayor Pete Buttigieg
subject: The view from South Bend
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
My name is Pete Buttigieg, and I'm the Mayor of South Bend, Indiana. When I first ran for office, many of the industries that once powered our city were gone, and they'd taken residents' livelihoods w |
---

| | Hi there Peter,

**My name is Pete Buttigieg, and I 'm the Mayor of South Bend, Indiana.** When I first ran for office, many of the industries that once powered our city were gone, and they'd taken residents' livelihoods with them. I wanted to bring opportunity back to South Bend, because to me, that's what public service is all about.

**But today, I want to tell you about another public servant hard at work for Indiana: Senator Joe Donnelly.** Joe represented South Bend in Congress before serving in the Senate, and his priorities are the same as mine. Joe has his finger on the pulse of working Hoosiers, and he fights for their interests every day in Washington.

**But Joe 's facing a big threat from out-of-state special interests who don't know Indiana like we do. Will you chip in to help him fight back? >>** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
---
**[Express donate $10>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2099/7377f838/3f1c2d2a/51f97a54/2186522038/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sbmayor?express_lane=true&refcode=20180507.2nd&amount=10")**
**[Express donate $25>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2099/7377f838/3f1c2d2a/51f97a57/2186522038/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sbmayor?express_lane=true&refcode=20180507.2nd&amount=25")**
**[Express donate $50>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2099/7377f838/3f1c2d2a/51f97a56/2186522038/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sbmayor?express_lane=true&refcode=20180507.2nd&amount=50")**
**[Express donate $100>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2099/7377f838/3f1c2d2a/51f97a51/2186522038/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sbmayor?express_lane=true&refcode=20180507.2nd&amount=100")**
**[Or, donate a different amount>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2099/7377f838/3f1c2d2a/51f97a50/2186522038/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sbmayor?refcode=20180507.2nd")**
There are countless examples of ways Joe has made a difference for Hoosiers, but I'll focus on the one that made the biggest impact on me.

While serving in Congress in 2007, Joe heard from veterans in the South Bend area that they had to travel way too far to receive health care, so he worked first to study the problem and then to solve it. **His persistence paid off: Last September, Joe was proud to speak at the ribbon cutting of a new VA clinic in St. Joseph County.**

Serving in the Navy, I saw firsthand the sacrifices our men and women in uniform make to defend our country. When people raise their right hand and promise to defend the United States with their lives, they enter into an agreement that we're going to take care of them as well -- so ensuring that veterans have access to health care is about honoring a commitment. **And Joe knows that we have to keep our commitments -- which is why he 's one of the Senate's best champions for Social Security, Medicare, and other programs Hoosiers rely on.**

We could use more leaders like Joe Donnelly -- but before we can get more, we need to re-elect the one we've got. [**Will you contribute to Joe 's re-election campaign so he can continue delivering results for Hoosiers?**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2099/7377f838/3f1c2d2a/51f97a53/2186522038/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sbmayor?refcode=20180507.2nd&amount=10")

Thanks, Peter -- together, we can send Joe back to the Senate.

Mayor Pete

**[**CONTRIBUTE**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2099/7377f838/3f1c2d2a/51f97a52/2186522038/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sbmayor?refcode=20180507.2nd&amount=25")**
